content.problemStatement:
Chef is stuck in the wavey world of polynomials. You are given all NNN roots of a polynomial P(x)=∏i=1N(x−ai)P(x) = \prod_{i=1}^N (x - a_i)P(x)=∏i=1N​(x−ai​). The roots are pairwise distinct integers, but they are not given in any particular order.

To help Chef escape, you should answer QQQ queries (numbered 111 through QQQ). For each valid iii, in the iii-th query, you are given an integer xix_ixi​ and you have to determine whether P(xi)P(x_i)P(xi​) is positive, negative or 000.
constraints: 1 <= N, Q <= 200000 (sum of N and Q over all test cases fits in memory limits)
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: first line contains two integers N and Q. The second line contains N distinct integers a1, a2, …, aN – the roots of the polynomial. Each of the next Q lines contains a single integer xi, the point at which the polynomial must be evaluated.
content.outputFormat: For each query, output a line containing one of the strings: "POSITIVE" if P(xi) > 0, "NEGATIVE" if P(xi) < 0, or "0" if P(xi) = 0.

solution.approach: The sign of P(x) is determined solely by the number of factors (x - ai) that are negative. After sorting the distinct roots, for a query x we locate the first root greater than x using binary search. If x equals a root, the polynomial value is zero. Otherwise, the count of roots greater than x equals the number of negative factors. If this count is even, the product of an even number of negative terms is positive; if odd, it is negative. Sorting takes O(N log N) and each query is answered in O(log N) time.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N + Q log N) per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for storing the sorted roots.
